record.field: Seafile is a high-performance, self-hosted file sync and share platform that keeps your data on your own infrastructure.

Seafile is a high-performance, self-hosted file sync and share platform that keeps your data on your own infrastructure.

Built around a distinctive library-based architecture that stores files as encrypted blocks, Seafile delivers fast incremental synchronization that transfers only changed content rather than whole files. It targets teams and organizations that need data sovereignty without giving up modern collaboration, offering cross-platform desktop, mobile, and virtual-drive clients alongside a Django-based web interface. A Community Edition under AGPLv3 covers the core features, with an optional proprietary Pro Edition for advanced enterprise needs.

Key features
  • Library-based storage with reliable, delta-only incremental sync and selective folder syncing
  • Client-side end-to-end encryption so even server administrators cannot read library contents
  • Virtual drive client that keeps files in the cloud and downloads them on demand
  • File sharing via links and groups with granular permissions, plus WebDAV access
  • Online document editing and collaboration through SeaDoc, OnlyOffice, or Collabora integration
  • LDAP/SSO authentication and cross-platform clients for Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS
Best for

Teams, businesses, and self-hosting enthusiasts that want a private, reliable alternative to public cloud storage for syncing, sharing, and collaborating on files with strong privacy controls.

services: seafile-db: image: mariadb:10.11 environment: - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=${INIT_SEAFILE_MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-} - MYSQL_LOG_CONSOLE=true - MARIADB_AUTO_UPGRADE=1 volumes: - ./seafile_mysql_db:/var/lib/mysql" networks: - internal healthcheck: test: - CMD - /usr/local/bin/healthcheck.sh - --connect - --mariadbupgrade - --innodb_initialized interval: 20s start_period: 30s timeout: 5s retries: 10 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_seafile_db' restart: unless-stopped memcached: image: memcached:1.6.29 entrypoint: memcached -m 256 networks: - internal healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - bash -c "echo version | (exec 3<>/dev/tcp/localhost/11211; cat >&3; timeout 0.5 cat <&3; exec 3<&-)" interval: 20s timeout: 5s retries: 10 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_memcached' restart: unless-stopped seafile: image: seafileltd/seafile-mc:12.0-latest volumes: - ./seafile_data:/shared environment: - DB_HOST=${SEAFILE_MYSQL_DB_HOST:-seafile-db} - DB_PORT=${SEAFILE_MYSQL_DB_PORT:-3306} - DB_USER=${SEAFILE_MYSQL_DB_USER:-seafile} - DB_ROOT_PASSWD=${INIT_SEAFILE_MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-} - DB_PASSWORD=${SEAFILE_MYSQL_DB_PASSWORD:?Variable is not set or empty} - SEAFILE_MYSQL_DB_CCNET_DB_NAME=${SEAFILE_MYSQL_DB_CCNET_DB_NAME:-ccnet_db} - SEAFILE_MYSQL_DB_SEAFILE_DB_NAME=${SEAFILE_MYSQL_DB_SEAFILE_DB_NAME:-seafile_db} - SEAFILE_MYSQL_DB_SEAHUB_DB_NAME=${SEAFILE_MYSQL_DB_SEAHUB_DB_NAME:-seahub_db} - TIME_ZONE=${TIME_ZONE:-Etc/UTC} - INIT_SEAFILE_ADMIN_EMAIL=${INIT_SEAFILE_ADMIN_EMAIL:-me@example.com} - INIT_SEAFILE_ADMIN_PASSWORD=${INIT_SEAFILE_ADMIN_PASSWORD:-asecret} - SEAFILE_SERVER_HOSTNAME=${SEAFILE_SERVER_HOSTNAME:?Variable is not set or empty} - SEAFILE_SERVER_PROTOCOL=${SEAFILE_SERVER_PROTOCOL:-http} - SITE_ROOT=${SITE_ROOT:-/} - NON_ROOT=${NON_ROOT:-false} - JWT_PRIVATE_KEY=${JWT_PRIVATE_KEY:?Variable is not set or empty} - SEAFILE_LOG_TO_STDOUT=${SEAFILE_LOG_TO_STDOUT:-true} depends_on: seafile-db: condition: service_healthy memcached: condition: service_started networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network healthcheck: test: - CMD - curl - -f - http://127.0.0.1:80/api2/ping interval: 20s timeout: 5s retries: 10 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T seafile-db sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d seafile-db # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T seafile-db sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
